description: Slide skill routing graph — maps user intent to the correct subdomain + presentation type

business_corporate/investment_finance.label: Investment & Finance
business_corporate/investment_finance.when: For investors, boards, or finance leadership. Raising money, reporting financials, M&A, budget approvals.
business_corporate/investment_finance.not: Internal ops updates (→ operations_reporting), strategy/planning (→ strategy_planning), selling to customers (→ sales)

board_deck.what: Quarterly/monthly board of directors meeting — company performance, decisions required, strategic updates, financial health.
board_deck.audience: Board of directors: independent directors, investor-appointed members.
board_deck.not_this: If presenting strategy to leadership (not board) → strategy_presentation. If exec briefing on one topic → executive_briefing (operations).

series_a_deck.what: Series A fundraising presentation — market opportunity, product differentiation, traction, team, investment thesis.
series_a_deck.audience: Series A investors: partners at institutional VC firms.
series_a_deck.not_this: If selling product to customers → sales_pitch_deck. If describing company to employees → culture_deck. 'I'm a Series A company' is context, not intent.

investor_pitch_seed_deck.what: Pre-seed or seed stage fundraising — team, market thesis, early product before substantial traction.
investor_pitch_seed_deck.not_this: If the startup is selling to customers → sales_pitch_deck. If later stage (Series B+) → fundraising_roadshow.

fundraising_roadshow.what: Polished investor pitch for formal fundraising — presented to multiple investors over a short period.
fundraising_roadshow.audience: Institutional VCs, growth equity, strategic investors, family offices.
fundraising_roadshow.not_this: If selling product to customers → sales_pitch_deck.

earnings_presentation.what: Quarterly/annual financial results for investors and analysts — performance vs guidance, drivers, forward guidance.
earnings_presentation.audience: Public market investors, equity analysts, institutional holders.
earnings_presentation.not_this: If internal performance review → quarterly_business_review_internal. If for board only → board_deck.

quarterly_business_review_internal.what: Internal QBR for senior leadership — company performance vs plan across all functions.
quarterly_business_review_internal.not_this: If reviewing WITH a customer → customer_qbr (sales). If for public investors → earnings_presentation. If for the board → board_deck.

analyst_investor_day.what: Multi-hour investor/analyst event — long-term strategy, financial model, market leadership.
analyst_investor_day.audience: Buy-side analysts, portfolio managers, sell-side equity analysts.

ma_due_diligence.what: Overview for a potential acquirer or investment committee — target's business, financials, strategic fit, diligence findings.
ma_due_diligence.audience: Investment committee, CEO/CFO, board M&A committee.

budget_proposal.what: Annual operating budget presentation — revenue targets, cost plans, investment priorities, headcount.
budget_proposal.not_this: If proposing one specific initiative → business_case_proposal (strategy). If asking investors for money → fundraising_roadshow.

business_corporate/operations_reporting.label: Operations & Reporting
business_corporate/operations_reporting.when: Internal operational updates. What's happening, what's blocked, how things are running. Status, reviews, retrospectives. Audience is INTERNAL teams/leadership.
business_corporate/operations_reporting.not: Investor or finance focused (→ investment_finance), strategy and planning (→ strategy_planning), selling to customers (→ sales)

all_hands_meeting.what: Company-wide meeting — performance updates, strategic context, team news, culture moments.
all_hands_meeting.audience: All employees across functions and levels.
all_hands_meeting.not_this: If only for leadership → executive_briefing. If about culture/values specifically → culture_deck (people).

executive_briefing.what: Concise high-stakes presentation to senior execs — inform a decision, update on critical situation.
executive_briefing.not_this: If for all employees → all_hands_meeting. If board-level → board_deck (investment). If strategic direction → strategy_presentation (strategy).

operational_review.what: Structured review of business operations — performance against operational KPIs, inefficiencies, improvement priorities.
operational_review.not_this: If financial/company-wide performance → quarterly_business_review_internal (investment). If project-specific → project_status_update.

partnership_update.what: Update on strategic partnership — joint activities, performance vs targets, relationship health.
partnership_update.audience: Internal exec team and/or partner organization's leadership.

post_mortem.what: Analysis of what went wrong after incident/failure — root cause analysis and process changes.
post_mortem.not_this: If about risk exposure broadly → risk_review.

project_status_update.what: Progress report on active project — milestones, blockers, budget/timeline health, decisions needed.
project_status_update.audience: Project sponsors, steering committee, exec leadership.
project_status_update.not_this: If about operations broadly → operational_review. If about product roadmap → product_roadmap (strategy).

rfp_response.what: Response to a formal Request for Proposal — capability, experience, commercial terms.
rfp_response.audience: Procurement committee, technical evaluators, scoring panel.
rfp_response.not_this: If a sales proposal (not responding to formal RFP) → proposal_presentation (sales).

risk_review.what: Assessment of key risks — exposure, controls, trajectory, actions required.
risk_review.audience: Board risk committee, audit committee, C-suite.
risk_review.not_this: If analyzing future scenarios → scenario_planning (strategy). If a specific incident → post_mortem.

vendor_review.what: Performance review of vendor/supplier — delivery against SLAs, costs, renewal decision.
vendor_review.not_this: If reviewing a customer relationship → customer_qbr (sales).

business_corporate/people_culture.label: People & Culture
business_corporate/people_culture.when: People, HR, culture, team, and organizational topics. Internal-facing, human-first. Brand identity/guidelines are marketing unless specifically about employer brand for recruiting.
business_corporate/people_culture.not: Marketing employer brand campaigns (→ marketing), strategy/planning (→ strategy_planning)

culture_deck.what: Company values, working principles, cultural identity — external employer branding or internal alignment.
culture_deck.audience: Candidates, new hires, all employees, leadership.
culture_deck.not_this: If recruiting-focused EVP → employer_branding. If company-wide news → all_hands_meeting (operations).

dei_presentation.what: Diversity, equity, inclusion — representation data, commitments, progress, business case.
dei_presentation.audience: Board, all-hands, external stakeholders, ERG leadership.

employer_branding.what: Employer value proposition (EVP) — what makes the company great to work at, talent acquisition strategy.
employer_branding.not_this: If about the company's product/visual brand → brand_presentation (marketing). If general culture → culture_deck.

onboarding_deck.what: New employee orientation — company, culture, tools, expectations, team.

org_design.what: Organizational design change — rationale, new structure, role impacts, transition plan.

performance_review.what: Performance review findings, cycle results, or new performance management framework.
performance_review.not_this: If about company financial performance → quarterly_business_review_internal (investment). If about operational KPIs → operational_review (operations).

team_offsite.what: Working session or strategic offsite — team alignment on goals, relationships, strategy.
team_offsite.audience: Leadership team, department, or cross-functional group.
team_offsite.not_this: If about OKR/goal planning specifically → okr_goal_setting (strategy).

business_corporate/strategy_planning.label: Strategy & Planning
business_corporate/strategy_planning.when: Internal business/product strategy and direction. What to build, where to compete, goals, priorities. Forward-looking, analytical. Audience is INTERNAL leadership/team.
business_corporate/strategy_planning.not: Marketing execution/content/campaigns/brand (→ marketing), operational status (→ operations_reporting), financial reporting (→ investment_finance). "Marketing strategy" is marketing, not strategy_planning.

business_case_proposal.what: Argument for allocating budget/resources to a specific initiative — problem, solution, expected return, risks.
business_case_proposal.not_this: If the whole annual budget → budget_proposal (investment). If selling to external customer → proposal_presentation (sales). If asking investors for money → fundraising_roadshow (investment).

competitive_analysis.what: Competitive landscape evaluation — key players, comparison dimensions, opportunities and threats.
competitive_analysis.not_this: If about winning a specific sales deal → competitive_battle_card (sales). If about M&A target → ma_due_diligence (investment).

go_to_market_strategy.what: Plan for entering a market, launching a product, or scaling GTM — ICP, channels, messaging, targets.
go_to_market_strategy.audience: CEO, board, VP Sales, VP Marketing.
go_to_market_strategy.not_this: If about the marketing campaign for the launch → product_launch_deck (marketing). If about the product itself → product_roadmap.

okr_goal_setting.what: OKR planning — introducing, cascading, or reviewing objectives and key results.
okr_goal_setting.not_this: If about team alignment at offsite → team_offsite (people).

product_roadmap.what: Time-horizoned view of what the product team will build and why.
product_roadmap.audience: Internal exec team, board, customer advisory boards.
product_roadmap.not_this: If about marketing a product launch → product_launch_deck (marketing). If about GTM for the product → go_to_market_strategy.

scenario_planning.what: Analysis of 2-4 plausible futures with revenue, cost, and strategic implications.
scenario_planning.audience: Board, risk committee, senior exec team.

strategy_presentation.what: Multi-year strategic plan — where the company is, where it's going, priorities and investments.
strategy_presentation.not_this: If about one specific initiative → business_case_proposal. If about product specifically → product_roadmap. If about marketing → marketing_strategy.

sales_marketing/marketing.label: Marketing
sales_marketing/marketing.when: Marketing execution: brand, campaigns, content, PR, launches, media, sponsorships, influencer partnerships. For EXTERNAL audiences (customers, press, partners, sponsors, creators, the public). Job titles like Brand Director, Marketing Manager, Head of Partnerships, Creative Strategist, Content Manager signal this category.
sales_marketing/marketing.not: Selling 1:1 to specific customers (→ sales), internal company strategy (→ strategy_planning), employer branding for recruitment (→ people_culture). "Marketing strategy" and "marketing plan" belong HERE, not in strategy_planning.

agency_pitch.what: Creative/digital agency pitching to win a client brief — strategy, creative approach, credentials.
agency_pitch.audience: Client-side: Marketing Director, CMO, Brand Director.
agency_pitch.not_this: If selling your own product → sales_pitch_deck (sales).

brand_presentation.what: New or refreshed brand identity — positioning, visual system, tone of voice.
brand_presentation.not_this: If about employer brand for recruiting → employer_branding (people_culture).

campaign_presentation.what: Marketing campaign — strategic brief, creative concept, channel plan, budget, measurement.
campaign_presentation.not_this: If about overall marketing strategy → marketing_strategy. If about content specifically → content_strategy.

content_strategy.what: Content plan — pillars, formats, channels, production plan, measurement.
content_strategy.not_this: If about overall business strategy → strategy_presentation (strategy). If about a specific campaign → campaign_presentation.

event_sponsorship_proposal.what: Business case for event sponsorship — audience reach, ICP alignment, pipeline potential, ROI.
event_sponsorship_proposal.audience: CEO, CFO, VP Marketing, budget committee.

influencer_partnership_brief.what: Brief for influencer campaign — objectives, creator criteria, content guidelines, budget, metrics.

marketing_strategy.what: Annual/multi-year marketing plan — strategic shift, channel mix, budget, team structure, targets.
marketing_strategy.not_this: If about one campaign → campaign_presentation. If about business strategy broadly → strategy_presentation (strategy). If about GTM → go_to_market_strategy (strategy).

media_kit.what: Sales document for advertisers/sponsors — audience profile, formats, pricing, social proof.
media_kit.audience: Advertising directors, media buyers, brand partnership managers.

pr_communications_strategy.what: Communications plan for a major announcement — narrative, outlets, message hierarchy, spokesperson, timeline.
pr_communications_strategy.not_this: If about internal communications to employees → all_hands_meeting or executive_briefing (operations). If about brand identity → brand_presentation.

product_launch_deck.what: Internal go-to-launch plan — launch strategy, channel plan, enablement, targets.
product_launch_deck.audience: CEO, CMO, VP Sales, VP Product.
product_launch_deck.not_this: If about the product roadmap → product_roadmap (strategy). If about the GTM strategy → go_to_market_strategy (strategy). If about marketing strategy broadly → marketing_strategy.

sales_marketing/sales.label: Sales
sales_marketing/sales.when: Selling to or managing external customers. Winning deals, demos, proposals, renewals, enablement, QBRs with clients, case studies. Job titles like Account Executive, Customer Success, Sales Director, Head of Sales signal this category.
sales_marketing/sales.not: Marketing campaigns (→ marketing), internal reporting (→ operations_reporting), raising money from investors (→ investment_finance). Customer-facing proposals are sales, not operations.

case_study_presentation.what: Customer success story for buyers — credibility in competitive evaluation with comparable results.
case_study_presentation.audience: CISO, technical evaluators, procurement, C-suite in active evaluation.

competitive_battle_card.what: Internal sales enablement — positioning, objection handling, tactics to win against a specific competitor.
competitive_battle_card.audience: Sales team: AEs, SDRs, sales management.
competitive_battle_card.not_this: If strategic competitive landscape analysis → competitive_analysis (strategy).

customer_qbr.what: Review meeting WITH existing customer — value delivered, gaps, next-quarter priorities, renewal/expansion.
customer_qbr.audience: VP Operations, Head of HR, or equivalent customer-side decision-maker.
customer_qbr.not_this: If internal financial QBR → quarterly_business_review_internal (investment_finance). If vendor we're reviewing → vendor_review (operations).

discovery_needs_analysis.what: Discovery/qualification call deck — frame conversation, confirm prospect's situation, qualify opportunity.
discovery_needs_analysis.audience: CISO, VP Engineering, CTO in early buying stages.

executive_sponsorship.what: Targeted C-suite pitch to establish executive sponsorship for a stalled deal.
executive_sponsorship.audience: CFO, CEO, COO — brought in by champion.
executive_sponsorship.not_this: If general sales pitch → sales_pitch_deck. If internal budget ask → business_case_proposal (strategy).

product_demo.what: Demo session combining deck with live product walkthrough — solving specific buyer pain points.
product_demo.not_this: If about the product roadmap internally → product_roadmap (strategy).

proposal_presentation.what: Formal commercial proposal — problem, solution, expected return, clear ask.
proposal_presentation.audience: C-suite and operational leadership comparing vendors.
proposal_presentation.not_this: If responding to a formal RFP → rfp_response (operations). If internal budget request → business_case_proposal (strategy). If internal investment → budget_proposal (investment).

renewal_upsell.what: Contract renewal conversation — value delivered, renewal commitment, expansion with additional product/seats.
renewal_upsell.audience: VP People, VP Operations — contract owner.
renewal_upsell.not_this: If reviewing customer relationship (not renewing) → customer_qbr.

sales_enablement.what: Internal presentation proposing or reviewing a sales enablement programme — ROI, design, budget.
sales_enablement.not_this: If training new employees broadly → onboarding_deck (people). If battle card for one competitor → competitive_battle_card.

sales_pitch_deck.what: Buyer-facing company story — problem, solution, case for purchase.
sales_pitch_deck.audience: VP Operations, VP CX, mid-market decision-makers.
sales_pitch_deck.not_this: If pitching to investors → fundraising_roadshow or series_a_deck (investment_finance). If agency pitching for a client → agency_pitch (marketing).

educational/academic.label: Educational & Academic
educational/academic.when: Academic presentations, research projects, university coursework, thesis defenses, conference talks, technical deep-dives on ML/AI/data science, scientific findings, capstone projects, or any educational/teaching material. Audience is professors, classmates, conference attendees, or academic reviewers.
educational/academic.not: Product pitches to customers (→ sales), internal company strategy (→ strategy_planning), marketing campaigns (→ marketing). A company presenting ML internally is operations or strategy, not educational.

ml_project_presentation.what: Machine learning or data science project presentation — methodology, model architecture, results, evaluation metrics, findings.
ml_project_presentation.audience: Professors, academic reviewers, classmates, conference attendees.
ml_project_presentation.not_this: If pitching an ML product to customers → sales_pitch_deck (sales). If internal ML strategy → strategy_presentation (strategy).

research_presentation.what: Academic research findings — literature review, methodology, results, discussion, future work.
research_presentation.audience: Conference attendees, thesis committee, peer reviewers, research group.
research_presentation.not_this: If about a product → product_roadmap (strategy). If competitive market research → competitive_analysis (strategy).

capstone_presentation.what: University capstone or final project — problem statement, approach, implementation, demo, results.

lecture_tutorial.what: Teaching or tutorial presentation — concept explanation, step-by-step walkthrough, educational content.
lecture_tutorial.not_this: If sales training → sales_enablement (sales). If employee onboarding → onboarding_deck (people_culture).

technical_deep_dive.what: In-depth technical presentation — architecture, algorithms, benchmarks, implementation details.
technical_deep_dive.audience: Engineers, researchers, technical reviewers, tech conference attendees.
technical_deep_dive.not_this: If product demo for customers → product_demo (sales). If internal sprint review → project_status_update (operations).

topic_explainer.what: General topic explainer or informational presentation — explain a subject, concept, phenomenon, or trend to a broad audience.
topic_explainer.audience: General audience, students, stakeholders wanting to understand a topic.
topic_explainer.not_this: If about a company's product → sales_pitch_deck (sales). If internal strategy → strategy_presentation (strategy). If formal academic research → research_presentation.

fallback.when: Generic company overviews, topic explainers, or requests that genuinely don't fit any specific category. Most requests DO fit a specific category — only use this as a last resort.

disambiguation.reason: Company stage ≠ presentation purpose. 'Series A company' ≠ 'Series A deck'.
Internal QBR → quarterly_business_review_internal. WITH customer → customer_qbr (sales).
Launch execution is marketing. Product roadmap planning is strategy.
Employer branding is people/culture. Product/visual brand is marketing.
Sales proposals go to customers. Budget proposals go to internal leadership.
Strategic competitive analysis vs. tactical battle card for winning a deal.
PR/comms strategy is marketing. Internal comms is operations.
Startup fundraising pitches are investment_finance, not the generic fallback.
Competitive analysis is strategy_planning/competitive_analysis, not a generic deck.
Internal budget asks are business_case_proposal, not investor pitches.
Product demos are sales/product_demo, not generic decks.
Sales-specific training is sales_enablement, not general onboarding.
Marketing plans are marketing execution, not corporate strategy. "Marketing strategy" = marketing_strategy type.
Brand identity is marketing unless explicitly about employer branding for recruiting. Rebrands are marketing.
Content planning is marketing execution, not corporate strategy.
QBR with a customer/client is sales. QBR for internal leadership is investment_finance.

disambiguation.prompt_pattern: Account Executive/Customer Success + presenting to customer/prospect
disambiguation.reason: AEs and CSMs sell to customers. Their role signals sales, not finance.
PR and press strategy targets external audiences = marketing. Only internal comms = operations.

disambiguation.prompt_pattern: sponsorship/sponsor an event/partnership proposal to external party
disambiguation.reason: Sponsorship asks and external partnership proposals are marketing, not internal strategy.

disambiguation.prompt_pattern: product launch/launching/feature launch/go to market launch
disambiguation.reason: Launches are marketing execution. Even vague "launching something new" is marketing.
Sales team training materials are sales enablement, not operations.
Renewals and upsells are customer-facing sales activities.
Discovery calls are early-stage sales activities.
Influencer and creator partnerships are marketing, not people/culture.
Press/media kits target external audiences = marketing.

disambiguation.prompt_pattern: case study/customer story/success story + competitive deal
disambiguation.reason: Customer case studies used in deals are sales tools, not finance.
Marketing campaigns are marketing execution, never generic general-deck.
Academic and research presentations belong in educational/academic, not the generic fallback.
Technical talks and conference presentations are educational, not internal ops updates.
Teaching and tutorial content is educational/academic, not a generic deck.

disambiguation.prompt_pattern: presentation about X/explain X/overview of X/introduction to X/what is X
disambiguation.reason: Topic explainers and informational presentations are educational/academic (topic_explainer type), not generic fallback.
